description: Securely back up and restore end-to-end encryption session keys. Keys are client-encrypted before upload; server stores only opaque ciphertext with versioned etag tracking.

actors.name: User Device
actors.description: Device uploading keys to backup or downloading them for recovery

actors.name: Homeserver
actors.description: Server storing encrypted backup data without access to plaintext keys

fields.name: backup_version
fields.label: Identifier of the active backup version

fields.name: etag
fields.label: Incrementing tag tracking the number of modifications to a backup version

fields.name: room_id
fields.label: Room whose session keys are being backed up

fields.name: session_id
fields.label: Identifier of the specific encryption session within a room

fields.name: session_data
fields.label: Encrypted session key payload; opaque to the server

fields.name: first_message_index
fields.label: Index of the earliest message this session key can decrypt

fields.name: forwarded_count
fields.label: Number of times this key has been forwarded between devices

fields.name: is_verified
fields.label: Whether the uploading device has verified the key's authenticity

values.description: This is the current backup version accepting uploads
A newer backup version has been created; this version is read-only

transitions.description: User creates a new backup version, transitioning the old one to superseded

integrity: Backup data is encrypted by the client before upload; server never has access to plaintext session keys
Uploads must specify the current backup version; mismatched versions are rejected
Read operations acquire a shared lock; write operations acquire an exclusive lock per user to prevent races
Version transitions are atomic; no writes are accepted to the old version after transition begins
The etag is incremented on every successful upload to allow clients to detect missed updates
isolation: Backup versions are per-user; multiple users' backups are fully isolated
Deleting a backup version removes all associated session key data irreversibly

given: no concurrent version transition is in progress

then.description: New backup version record created with initial etag of 0

backup_version_created.result: New backup version is active and accepts key uploads

given: uploaded version matches the current active version

then.description: Session keys merged into backup; etag incremented
Etag incremented to reflect the change

keys_uploaded.result: Keys are safely stored; client can discard local copy

given: requester is authenticated as the backup owner
keys_retrieved.result: Encrypted session keys returned; client decrypts and restores local encryption state

given: uploaded version does not match the current active backup version
version_mismatch.result: Upload rejected; client should retrieve the current version and retry

given: requested backup version does not exist
backup_not_found.result: Operation fails; client should create a new backup version

given: user requests deletion of a backup version

then.description: All session keys for the version removed; version record deleted

backup_version_deleted.result: Backup version and all its key data permanently removed

errors.message: The specified backup version does not match the current version

events.name: key_backup.version_created
events.description: A new key backup version has been created

events.name: key_backup.keys_uploaded
events.description: Session keys were successfully uploaded to a backup version

events.name: key_backup.keys_retrieved
events.description: Encrypted session keys were retrieved for recovery

events.name: key_backup.version_deleted
events.description: A backup version and all its key data were permanently removed

related.reason: Session keys generated during key exchange are the data being backed up
Cross-signing trust level of the uploading device is recorded in is_verified

goals.description: Securely back up and restore end-to-end encryption session keys. Keys are client-encrypted before upload; server stores only opaque ciphertext with versioned etag tracking.

constraints.description: Sensitive fields must be encrypted at rest and never logged in plaintext

human_checkpoints: before transitioning to a terminal state

tradeoffs.reason: authentication must prioritize preventing unauthorized access

invariants: sensitive fields are never logged in plaintext
all data access is authenticated and authorized
error messages never expose internal system details
state transitions follow the defined state machine — no illegal transitions
